status: S376 predictions MIXED (cumulative on-track, era-specific WORSENED)

cumulative.note: Peaked at S350, declining since. On track for <0.45 by ~S430.

era_specific.note: UCB1 is MORE concentrated within its own era than pre-UCB1 was.

rolling_50.note: S358-S375 was best (cooldown+saturation fresh). S375+ reverted.

coverage.ucb1_era_only: 19/40 (48%) — half of domains get ZERO in 14 sessions

quality.note: Quality UP despite coverage DOWN. UCB1 selects productive domains.

visit_gini_lt_045.trajectory: Cumulative on-track for ~S430. Era-specific will NOT meet target.
visit_gini_lt_045.note: S376 predicted cumulative, which is improving but slower than expected.

key_finding: TWO-SPEED COVERAGE PARADOX: UCB1 improves cumulative Gini (0.625→0.520) by shifting exploitation clusters between eras, while era-specific Gini worsens (0.587→0.752). Within any 14-session window, 52% of domains get zero visits. This is by design — UCB1 minimizes cumulative regret, not per-window balance. Practical impact: domain knowledge decays for abandoned domains, but merge rate and yield improve because exploitation targets productive domains.
root_cause: UCB1 explore term = sqrt(log(N)/n_i). Domains with high historical visits (linguistics=15) have small explore bonuses. Outcome feedback (PROVEN → low bonus per L-654) further reduces incentive. Domains rationally abandoned are historically well-explored but low-current-yield.
recommendation: Accept two-speed coverage as feature, not bug. If domain attrition is concerning, add a REVIVAL mechanism: every ~30 sessions, force one dispatch to the longest-dormant domain. Alternatively, increase c parameter from 1.414 to ~2.0 to weight exploration more.
